title: National Museum of Play Recognition Act
short_title: National Museum of Play Recognition Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.
plain_summary: This bill gives the Margaret Woodbury Strong Museum in Rochester, New York a special title as the National Museum of Play. The museum gets this honor because it is the only museum in the country that focuses on studying how playing helps kids learn and be creative. Even though it gets this national title, the museum will not become part of the National Park System and will not receive any money from the federal government. The recognition is just an official way to honor the museum's important work with play and learning.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the National Museum of Play Recognition Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Designation of National Museum of Play in Rochester, New York
sections.text: Congress officially recognizes that the Margaret Woodbury Strong Museum in Rochester, New York is special. This museum is also called the Strong Museum. It is the only museum that focuses only on studying how play helps people learn, be creative, and make discoveries. The museum also shows how play connects to our cultural history. Because of this unique mission, Congress officially names the Margaret Woodbury Strong Museum as the National Museum of Play. However, this special recognition does not make the museum part of the National Park System. The federal government will not be required to give money to the museum, and this recognition does not allow the museum to ask for federal money.
